files.title: Philosophy
files.description: The memo system rests on a small set of convictions about how an autonomous agent and a developer should work together: that planning outranks coding, that context is finite and must be spent...

files.title: Memo-SOP
files.description: `memo-sop` is the canonical entry skill of the memo system: the one document that explains the whole process end to end. This chapter defines what makes it the single source of truth, why it is the...

files.title: Input Paths
files.description: Every input to the memo system arrives as a transcript, and a mandatory header on its first line selects the flow that follows. This chapter defines the four transcript types the system recognizes...

files.title: Input Pipeline
files.description: Before any memo is created or any revision written, every input passes through a fixed, strictly ordered processing pipeline. This chapter defines that five-step pipeline, the order it runs in, and...

files.title: Memo Strategies
files.description: A memo is classified along two independent dimensions that must not be conflated: its *type* (where the SOP stops) and its *strategy* (how the work is shaped on the way there). This chapter specifies...

files.title: Memo Structure
files.description: A memo has a stable on-disk shape: a numbered directory, a project prefix to avoid ID collisions, and an internal layout that the rest of the system relies on. This chapter defines how memos are...

files.title: Revisions
files.description: A memo evolves through revisions, and each revision carries the strict handover surface from the AI to the downstream machinery. This chapter defines the three-area revision structure and the...

files.title: Phases & PRDs
files.description: A finalized memo becomes executable work through a chain of decompositions: topics become PRDs, PRDs are bundled and sequenced into phases, and a phase carries an orchestration role. This chapter...

files.title: Contamination & Handover
files.description: A memo or a session handover written from a degraded context carries its mis-weighting forward into the next document. The cause is **context rot** (quality decay of LLM output as input length...

files.title: Proactive Research
files.description: A memo is a planning artifact, and planning is the most important part of agile engineering. A plan built on unverified assumptions inherits every error those assumptions carry. Proactive research...

files.title: Quality Gates & Finalization
files.description: Quality is enforced by gates, and finalization is the readiness gate between strategy and implementation. This chapter defines the five quality skills, the six evidence levels, the finalization gate...

files.title: Rollout
files.description: The rollout turns a finalized memo into implemented code. It begins only **after** finalization (memo status "Finalized" or "Conditionally finalized") and only for memos of type Implementation —...

files.title: Orchestration
files.description: A rollout phase is executed by an **agent team**, not by a single linear pass. An orchestrator coordinates the team, persists progress to state files, and recovers exactly from a crash. This chapter...

files.title: Agents, Skills and Tasks
files.description: The system is moving toward **Agents as a base layer** on which skills build. This is not an abolition of skills: skills remain, and they remain the foundation for most procedures. Only the...

files.title: Prompt Generator
files.description: A sub-agent's starting point must be exactly reproducible. Non-deterministic prompt generation drifts a little with every unit, and after hundreds of units nobody can say which sub-agent started from...

files.title: Git & Security
files.description: After a rollout stop, the system has historically handed work over to an uncontrolled commit-and-push — "it runs out of control". This chapter fixes a **deterministic git flow** so that what happens...

files.title: Git Workflow and Memo IDs
files.description: Git is used here as **backup and findability**, not as a deployment trigger. The memo ID gives every work package a stable identifier across three levels (memo → phase → PRD), and a fixed mapping...

files.title: Multidimensionality
files.description: A single memo can coordinate work across **multiple repositories** at once. This is the multidimensional property of the memo system: the memo is the planning unit, and a planning unit naturally...

files.title: Communication
files.description: The system ingests its own working material — long dictated transcripts and the memos derived from them — as an **internal working basis**. That material is written for the author and the agent, in...

files.title: Flow: Full vs. Update Revisions
files.description: The memo flow runs from the first input to the rollout in one continuous shape: input processing produces the seed, `memo-init` writes the first full revision, a revision loop iterates until the memo...

files.title: Human-Computer Interaction
files.description: The interaction model fixes the **two points** where the user touches the system — input and feedback/finalization — and the autonomous span in between. Before finalization, the user's judgement...

files.title: Self-Describing Command Tree (Branch/Leaf)
files.description: The recommended way to expose tools to an agent is a **self-describing command tree**: a small tree of **branches** ("bags of tools") and **leaves** (tools that do something). Each leaf carries...

files.title: Requirements

files.title: Tools Registry
files.description: A memo lists the phases and PRDs it will execute, but the tools those phases assume are easy to leave implicit. They live in the author's memory and in the wider skill catalog, never written down...

files.title: Strands

files.title: Memo History
files.description: Memos in a project do not stand alone. They accumulate into a **cumulative timeline**: memo 0 is followed by memo 1, then memo 2, and so on, and read in order they tell the story of how a project...

files.title: Landing the Plane
files.description: Landing the plane is the fourth step of the rollout, after Generate, Execute, and Evaluate. Where Evaluate decides whether the work is correct, landing decides whether the workspace is in a state...

files.title: Drift
files.description: Drift is treated as its own error class, distinct from a normal bug. It deserves a dedicated chapter because the usual bug-fixing instinct — find the broken copy, fix it, move on — actively makes...

files.title: Behavioral Guardrails
files.description: The technical building blocks of this specification — orchestration, requirements, gates — only produce good outcomes if the agent driving them also behaves correctly. The guardrails in this chapter...
